svg.title: Angular HttpClient interceptor chain
svg.desc: 8 interceptors stacked vertically. Request flows top-down on the left spine. Response flows bottom-up on the right spine. Error path loops from I8 back to I7 retry.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does Angular HttpClient build the initial request before passing it to the interceptor chain?')
sendPrompt('What does correlationInterceptor add to requests and how does it push to CorrelationContext?')
sendPrompt('Why does securityInterceptor scope X-XSRF-TOKEN only to /api/* paths and not all requests?')
sendPrompt('How does cacheInterceptor decide whether to serve from cache or pass through? How does X-Cache-TTL opt-in work?')
g.text: GET + X-Cache-TTL header opt-in · 200-cap LRU store

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does dedupInterceptor implement single-flight for GET requests — does it share the same Observable or replay the response?')
g.text: GET single-flight · same cache key · releases on response

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does loadingInterceptor integrate with LoadingService signals and what does X-Skip-Loading opt-out look like?')
sendPrompt('What should the loggingInterceptor record — method, URL, status, duration — and how should it attach the correlation ID?')
sendPrompt('How does retryInterceptor implement exponential backoff with ±25% jitter and which HTTP methods does it allow retrying?')
sendPrompt('How does errorInterceptor normalize errors to ApiError, when does it toast vs navigate, and how does it distinguish session expiry from a resource 401?')
sendPrompt('What headers does Browser fetch automatically attach — Cookie, Accept-Encoding — that the interceptors do not need to set?')
